item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Helps with your beauty sleep | Sleep in quality, wake in confidence with Courts overseas beds
item.description: A good night’s sleep is the foundation of a healthier, happier life, and it all begins with the right bed. We understand that comfort is more than just a luxury it’s a necessity.
item.content:encoded:
A good night’s sleep is the foundation of a healthier, happier life, and it all begins with the right bed.

We understand that comfort is more than just a luxury it’s a necessity.

Whether you’re furnishing a new home, upgrading your bedroom, or creating a welcoming space for guests, Overseas Beds offers the perfect combination of quality, comfort, durability, and style to transform the way you sleep.

Crafted using premium-quality materials and designed with precision, every Overseas Bed is built to provide exceptional support and long-lasting performance.

Our commitment to quality ensures that each bed delivers the comfort you deserve, night after night, helping you wake up feeling refreshed, energized, and ready to take on the day. Why Choose Overseas Bed?

Superior Craftsmanship: Every Overseas Bed is manufactured using high-grade materials that are carefully selected for strength, durability, and lasting comfort. Built to withstand daily use, our beds are designed to remain supportive and reliable for years to come.

Exceptional Comfort: Designed with ergonomic principles in mind, Overseas Beds provide balanced body support that helps reduce pressure points and promotes proper sleeping posture. Whether you’re a side, back, or stomach sleeper, you’ll enjoy a more restful and uninterrupted night’s sleep.

Modern & Elegant Designs: Your bedroom should be a place of relaxation and style.

Overseas Bed offers a wide range of contemporary and timeless designs that effortlessly complement any bedroom décor. From minimalist aesthetics to classic finishes, there’s a bed to suit every taste and lifestyle.

Affordable Luxury: Premium comfort doesn’t have to come with a premium price tag.

Overseas Bed delivers exceptional value by combining superior quality with competitive pricing, making luxurious sleep accessible for every household. Easy Maintenance: Designed for everyday living,

Overseas Beds are easy to clean and maintain, allowing you to keep your bedroom looking fresh and inviting with minimal effort.

Perfect for homes, apartments, hotels, guest houses, and rental properties, Overseas Bed is trusted for its quality, reliability, and outstanding comfort. Whether you’re purchasing your first bed or upgrading to a better sleep experience, you can rest assured that you’re investing in furniture designed to enhance your everyday life.

Don’t settle for restless nights or uncomfortable sleep. Experience the difference that premium craftsmanship and thoughtful design can make.

Visit your nearest Courts store today and discover the complete range of Overseas Beds. Sleep Better. Live Better. Choose Overseas Bed Smart Credit Options

With some of the best credit deals in town, Courts makes it easy for families across Fiji to bring home quality furniture without stretching their budgets.

Flexible payment options allow more households to enjoy premium comfort and modern living while paying at a pace that suits them.

Visit your nearest Courts showroom today to explore the latest range of Overseas Beds.

Discover exceptional value, modern designs, and quality craftsmanship that will help transform your bedroom into what you’ve always wanted.

Stay connected with Courts by scanning the QR code for the latest promotions, OB and Red Pen Deal locations, and exclusive offers. You can also download the myCourts App to browse featured products, access your account, receive customer support, and stay up to date with the latest deals all from your mobile device.

Designed for everyday living, Overseas Beds are easy to clean and maintain, all available at Courts.

Sleep in quality with overseas beds at Courts.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | A mother’s journey to health, freedom, and purpose
item.description: At 41, Lite Daunika from Naluwai in Naitasiri is redefining what it means to be a modern Fijian mother. A stay-at-home mum of four, two teenagers in high school and two younger children in primary, her story is one that people can relate to filled with, quite determination, adaption and resilience. “I’ve been working most of my life,” she explains. “From a young age, I went straight into full-time
item.content:encoded:
At 41, Lite Daunika from Naluwai in Naitasiri is redefining what it means to be a modern Fijian mother. A stay-at-home mum of four, two teenagers in high school and two younger children in primary, her story is one that people can relate to filled with, quite determination, adaption and resilience.

“I’ve been working most of my life,” she explains. “From a young age, I went straight into full-time work, and for years it felt like I was just living from paycheque to paycheque.”

That cycle eventually pushed her to look overseas for better opportunities. Yet even after spending time abroad, the financial breakthrough she had hoped for did not materialise.

Everything changed when she discovered an online side hustle.

“I found something that allowed me to work from my phone, and it gave me the chance to come back home and still earn. That was my ticket back to my children.”Today, Lite runs her own online business as a high-ticket affiliate marketer. More importantly, she has reclaimed something she once missed, time with her family.

“It’s a real blessing, to be there when my kids leave for school and to pick them up afterward. That feeling is priceless.”

But beyond financial independence, Lite has embraced another role: what could be called a “shopper angel” within her household. Her approach to shopping and food is intentional, rooted in health and cultural values.

“When I was overseas, fast food was everywhere, it was convenient, but the nutrition just wasn’t there. Coming back home changed my mindset completely.”

Now, her weekly shopping routine revolves around fresh produce. Every Friday, she visits the local market with her children in tow.

“The market is my favourite place, I always start with vegetables, whatever I can afford and then I buy the extras from the supermarket.”

Her weekly grocery budget has risen from around $150 to $200–$250, reflecting the increasing cost of living. Still, she prioritises quality over convenience.

“You are what you eat, so if I want my children to do well in school and stay healthy, I have to make sure they’re eating properly.”

Her meals often include local staples such as root crops and leafy greens, complemented by simple additions like tuna for flavour. She also prepares fresh juices in the mornings and ensures her children carry balanced lunches to school every day.

“I always include a fruit or vegetable, even something small like carrots or cucumber makes a difference.”

Lite draws inspiration from other mothers as well.

“I follow a lady online who packs colourful, healthy lunchboxes for her kids, it really motivates me to do better.”

Health in her household goes beyond food. Herbal drinks, often prepared by her mother, play a regular role.

“Even when we’re not sick, we drink them, this helps in keeping our immune system strong.”

Looking ahead, she hopes to instil self-sufficiency in her children by encouraging them to plant their own food.

“We have land, so why not use it. “I want my children to understand the value of growing what we eat.”

Her advice to other mothers is simple and powerful.

“Start small. Add some fruit, add a vegetable. Drink more water. Look after yourself first because if you’re not well, everything else suffers.”

In Lite’s world, shopping is no longer just a routine task. It is an act of care, a strategy for wellbeing, and a foundation for a better future.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Living life the healthy way
item.description: AT 41, Lite Daunika of Naluwai, Naitasiri, is showing that success isn’t just measured by income, it’s measured by the life you build for your family. Once trapped in the cycle of living from paycheque to paycheque and searching overseas for better opportunities, the mother of four found an online business that brought her home while allowing her to earn an income.
item.content:encoded:
AT 41, Lite Daunika of Naluwai, Naitasiri, is showing that success isn’t just measured by income, it’s measured by the life you build for your family.

Once trapped in the cycle of living from paycheque to paycheque and searching overseas for better opportunities, the mother of four found an online business that brought her home while allowing her to earn an income.

Today, she is not only a successful digital entrepreneur but also a passionate advocate for healthy, affordable family living.

From choosing fresh market produce over processed food to teaching her children the value of growing their own crops, Lite’s journey is one of resilience, reinvention and purpose.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | More than just a cleaning product
item.description: FOR over 95 years, Domestos has been at the forefront of home hygiene, becoming a trusted household name and building on their purpose to ensure that clean and safe toilets ae a reality for everyone. Domestos flagship product, Domestos thick bleach, ensures it kills germs in hard-to-reach places, delivering an impressive cleaning performance while also being 100% biodegradable.
item.content:encoded:
FOR over 95 years, Domestos has been at the forefront of home hygiene, becoming a trusted household name and building on their purpose to ensure that clean and safe toilets ae a reality for everyone.

Domestos flagship product, Domestos thick bleach, ensures it kills germs in hard-to-reach places, delivering an impressive cleaning performance while also being 100% biodegradable.

By harnessing the power of what they do, the Domestos UNICEF partnership has helped over 29 million people gain access to cleaner and safer toilets, with the set goal of helping 100 million people.

Domestos has partnered with governments, NGO’s and development organizations to make school toilets cleaner and safer in countries such as Turkey, Vietnam, the Philippines and South Africa.

Domestos bleach represents itself as more than just a cleaning product. It is a symbol of trust, innovation and social responsibility.

In an era where hygiene has become a cornerstone of public health, Domestos continues to stand out for its efficacy, consumer trust, and purpose driven initiatives.

A product of Unilever, Domestos Bleach is distributed in Fiji by Motibhai Group, Sharing quality, sharing happiness.

Domestos Bleach is distributed in Fiji by the Motibhai Group. Pictures: Motibhai

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Save time and money
item.description: WITH the cost of living continuing to rise, many families are looking for practical ways to stretch their grocery budgets without compromising on quality. Fortunately, a few simple shopping habits can help you save both time and money while making your weekly supermarket trip more efficient.
item.content:encoded:
WITH the cost of living continuing to rise, many families are looking for practical ways to stretch their grocery budgets without compromising on quality.

Fortunately, a few simple shopping habits can help you save both time and money while making your weekly supermarket trip more efficient.

Whether you’re picking up a handful of essentials or doing a full week’s grocery run, planning ahead can make all the difference.

One of the biggest mistakes shoppers make is visiting the supermarket during peak hours, especially on Sunday afternoons.

After church services and before the start of a new week, supermarkets are often packed with customers.

Busy aisles, long checkout queues and limited choices of fresh produce can make shopping stressful and time-consuming.

Instead, try scheduling your major grocery shopping during quieter periods, such as early in the morning, later in the evening or on weekdays.

Shopping when stores are less crowded allows you to browse comfortably, compare prices and make thoughtful purchasing decisions without feeling rushed.

Another golden rule is to always shop with a list.

It may seem simple but writing down exactly what you need before leaving home is one of the easiest ways to avoid overspending and reduce food waste.

Smart shoppers save both time and money. Picture: GOOGLE

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Resetting the family budget
item.description: Bula Vinaka everyone, There is one place where almost every Fijian family feels the pinch of rising living costs and that is at the supermarket checkout. Whether you’re shopping for a week’s groceries or just picking up a few essentials, chances are you’ve looked at the total at the check out and wondered how it climbed so high compared to the last visit. A trolley that once comfortably fed the fa
item.content:encoded:
Bula Vinaka everyone, There is one place where almost every Fijian family feels the pinch of rising living costs and that is at the supermarket checkout.

Whether you’re shopping for a week’s groceries or just picking up a few essentials, chances are you’ve looked at the total at the check out and wondered how it climbed so high compared to the last visit. A trolley that once comfortably fed the family for the week now seems to cost much more, forcing many households to rethink how they spend every dollar.

This is before you visit the market and the fish shops! While we can’t control inflation or global food prices, we can control the decisions we make every time we shop. Sometimes, though, trimming a few dollars here and there isn’t enough.

If your household budget has drifted off course, it may be time for a financial reset. Around the world, a growing movement known as “No Buy 2026” is encouraging people to take a break from unnecessary spending.

Participants commit to buying only essentials for a set period, whether that’s for a week, a month or even a year. The aim isn’t to deprive yourself, but to become more aware of where your money is really going. For many families, a “Low Buy” approach may be more realistic.

Rather than cutting out everything, you simply set clear limits. Perhaps you decide there will be no new clothes unless they’re replacing worn-out items. Maybe takeaway meals become a once-a-month treat instead of a weekly habit.

These small changes can add up surprisingly quickly.

One of the biggest money drains isn’t always the expensive purchases, it’s the little ones. A snack here, an extra drink there or a few items picked up without a shopping list can quietly add hundreds of dollars to your annual spending.

Planning ahead is one of the simplest ways to save. Before heading to the supermarket, check what’s already in your pantry and freezer. Build your meals around ingredients you already have and write a shopping list based on what you genuinely need.

Shopping with a plan also reduces food waste, which is like throwing money straight into the rubbish bin. Buying local produce whenever possible is another smart choice.

Seasonal fruits and vegetables sold at municipal markets are often fresher and more affordable than imported alternatives. Staples such as dalo, tavioka, rourou and bele can provide nutritious, filling meals without stretching the family budget.

It also helps to remember why you’re making these changes. Perhaps you’re paying off a loan, building an emergency fund, saving for your children’s education or planning for your first home. Keeping that goal in mind makes it easier to resist impulse purchases.

And don’t forget that some of life’s best moments don’t come with a price tag. A family picnic, an afternoon at the beach, a walk in the park or a movie night at home can create memories just as valuable as an expensive day out or time out at a hotel. If you slip up, don’t be discouraged.

One unplanned purchase doesn’t undo all your progress. Every budget has its setbacks. The important thing is to recognise them, learn from them and keep moving forward.

At the end of the day, smart shopping isn’t about denying ourselves everything we enjoy. It’s about spending with purpose. Every dollar we save today is a dollar that can help build a stronger financial future tomorrow. Before leaving home, compare weekly specials in the supermarkets, on online or on Facebook from supermarkets such as Extra Supermarket and MaxVal-U, Superstore, NewWorld IGA, Rajendras or Shop N Save.

A few minutes of planning can save you money, reduce impulse buying and help you stick to your budget every time you shop.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Make everyday laundry effortless
item.description: Laundry is part of everyday life, but it does not have to take up your entire day. Panasonic brings together smart technology, trusted reliability, and thoughtful design to help every wash become quicker, easier, and more efficient.
item.content:encoded:
Laundry is part of everyday life, but it does not have to take up your entire day.

Panasonic brings together smart technology, trusted reliability, and thoughtful design to help every wash become quicker, easier, and more efficient.

Whether you are caring for a busy family, tackling large weekly loads, or looking after delicate garments, Panasonic offers a laundry solution that fits perfectly into your home and lifestyle.

Panasonic fully automatic washing machines make everyday washing effortless.

Simply load your clothes, select the right program, and let advanced washing technology do the rest.

Powerful wash performance removes stubborn dirt while treating fabrics with care, helping clothes stay cleaner, fresher, and looking their best after every wash.

Multiple wash programs, large capacities, and energy-efficient operation make these machines ideal for growing families, busy households, and anyone who values convenience without compromising on cleaning performance. Quiet operation also means laundry can run comfortably any time of the day.

Some homes appreciate the flexibility and speed of a Twin Tub Washing Machine. Panasonic Twin Tub Washers continue to be a trusted favorite across Fiji thanks to their dependable performance and practical design. Separate wash and spin tubs allow you to wash one load while spinning another, helping reduce laundry time and making large washing days easier to manage. Built with durable construction, powerful washing action, and generous capacity, these machines are well suited for family homes where reliability matters every single day.

Rainy weather and busy schedules no longer have to delay your laundry. Panasonic Clothes Dryers provide fast, gentle, and efficient drying whenever you need it. Carefully designed drying programs help protect fabrics while leaving clothes soft, dry, and ready to wear. Whether it is school uniforms, work clothes, towels, or bedding, a Panasonic Dryer delivers dependable performance that saves time and removes the worry of unpredictable weather. It is the perfect companion to your Panasonic washing machine, giving you complete laundry convenience from start to finish.

Every Panasonic laundry appliance is built with quality, durability, and everyday practicality in mind. Modern styling complements today’s homes while innovative features help simplify one of life’s regular household tasks. Less time doing laundry means more time enjoying the moments that matter most with family and friends. Whether you are replacing an older machine, upgrading your laundry, or furnishing a new home, Panasonic offers dependable solutions designed to make everyday living easier.

Discover the difference trusted Japanese engineering can bring to your home and experience laundry care that works as hard as you do.

Quality, convenience, and long-lasting performance come together in every Panasonic washer and dryer available at Narhari. 📍 Available at Narhari Stores: Suva • Lautoka • Nadi • Ba • Yadua-Sigatoka • Rakiraki • Nausori • Navua • Savusavu • Nakasi • Labasa. Discover more at: https://www.narharifiji.com/ Follow us for the latest deals, promos & tech updates: Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/narhari.fiji Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/narhari.fj TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@narhari_officialaccount Narhari – Quality solutions for your every room.

Find the perfect Panasonic washer and make every wash easier from day one.

Bring home effortless laundry days with this sleek Panasonic heat pump clothes dryer.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: MUSIC | A journey to the world stage
item.description: LONG before international chart placements, radio airplay and millions of streams, Tahi Johnson Fiu was a little boy with a guitar placed gently into his hands. Encouraged by his family, he was introduced to the instrument long before he imagined a career in the lucrative yet cutthroat music industry.
item.content:encoded:
LONG before international chart placements, radio airplay and millions of streams, Tahi Johnson Fiu was a little boy with a guitar placed gently into his hands.

Encouraged by his family, he was introduced to the instrument long before he imagined a career in the lucrative yet cutthroat music industry.

His first guitar became more than a childhood gift. It was a tool that ignited a lifelong passion that continues to grow.

As the years passed, he expanded his musicianship to the bass, piano and other instruments, driven by an endless curiosity not only to perform music but to understand how it was written, arranged and produced.

Growing up as a proud Rotuman-Fijian living in Australia, Fiu learnt that talent alone would never be adequate. Discipline, humility and respect were just as important as technical ability.

Countless hours were spent practising, listening and refining his craft, laying the foundation for a journey that would eventually carry him well beyond Pacific shores.

That commitment was rewarded when he earned admission to the prestigious Berklee College of Music in Boston, Massachusetts.

Today, his time at Berklee stands as the launching pad for an international career built not under the spotlight, but behind the scenes where producers, composers and session musicians quietly shape the music heard around the world.

Since graduating, Fiu has steadily established himself as a respected producer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist and bassist, working with artists across the United States, Europe, Asia and Australia.

His productions have reached audiences well beyond Fiji, with songs he produced accumulating more than 1.9 million Spotify streams across three titles alone.

The artists he has collaborated with collectively boast catalogues that have attracted hundreds of millions of streams worldwide.

Among his most recognised credits is Favela by Dublin rapper KhakiKid, where Fiu laid down the song’s signature bass and guitar parts.

The single climbed to No.10 on the Irish Homegrown Chart compiled by the Official Charts Company and remained in the Top 20 for five consecutive weeks.

It also earned airplay on Ireland’s leading broadcasters, including iRadio, Spin 1038 and the national broadcaster RTÉ 2fm, introducing Fiu’s musicianship to listeners across the country.

His growing reputation continued in the United States through his work on Sorry 2 Disappoint You, the album by Grammy-nominated songwriter Ré Lxuise.

Serving as composer, producer, writer and multi-instrumentalist, Fiu helped deliver a project that reached No.24 on both the Songstats United States and Global R&B/Soul Album charts, while also charting in Finland, Qatar and Azerbaijan.

Tracks from the album received rotation on radio stations in the United States, London and Finland, further expanding his international footprint.

In October 2023, Fiu also provided assistant production for Ré Lxuise’s opening performance for Macy Gray and California Jet Club at Atlanta’s renowned Eastern Theatre.

His portfolio continues to grow through collaborations with internationally recognised artists.

His production and instrumental work with kidsnot$aints has connected him to an artist whose catalogue has surpassed 42 million Spotify streams.

His bass and guitar performances feature on Donny Daydream’s VACANCY EP from Hong Kong, while he has also recorded with acclaimed Korean guitarist Rock Choi, an endorsed artist for D’Angelico Guitars, Jim Dunlop and Electro-Harmonix.Live performance has remained equally important throughout his journey.

Together with fellow musician Timothee Alex, Fiu formed the duo T&T, performing at Berklee’s inaugural Grad Fest at the House of Blues in Boston in 2023.

He has also shared stages internationally, including performances supporting Australian artist KIMMI at Sydney’s Oxford Art Factory.

What is unusual about Fiu’s story, viewed from Suva, is not that a musician with Rotuman roots has reached the international stage—Fiji has produced globally recognised artists for generations. Rather, it is that he has built his career from the production chair.

Bass players, producers and session musicians are rarely the names printed in the biggest headlines. More often, they are the creative force whose work appears in the credits rather than on the marquee.

The recognition accumulating around Fiu’s name has come from chart compilers, broadcasters and editorial curators within the music industry itself, reflecting the respect he has earned among professionals.

For young Fijian and Rotuman musicians who dream not only of performing but also of writing, arranging and producing music, Fiu’s career offers a tangible example of what is possible.

His growing catalogue demonstrates that success in the modern music industry extends well beyond the stage and into the recording studio, where songs are imagined, shaped and brought to life.

Now based in Los Angeles, Fiu continues to collaborate with artists, producers and songwriters from around the world, including Sony songwriter and producer Raphael George, while expanding a career that continues to connect Fiji to the international music industry.

Despite the growing list of achievements, chart successes and global collaborations, Fiu remains grounded in the values that first inspired his journey.
